---
title: "1. Data Quality Assessment"
---

::: {#0dd55532 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../src")
import utils

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import seaborn as sns

utils.set_theme()

dfs = utils.load_all()
baseline  = dfs["baseline"]
schools   = dfs["schools"]
math_chat = dfs["math_chat"]
math_quiz = dfs["math_quiz"]
voc_chat  = dfs["voc_chat"]
voc_steps = dfs["voc_steps"]
```
:::


## 1.1 Per-file Completeness

Missing values by dataset: proportion of cells missing per variable, and flagging variables above key thresholds.

::: {#3c0479f5 .cell execution_count=2}
``` {.python .cell-code}
def missing_summary(df: pd.DataFrame, name: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    n = len(df)
    missing = df.isnull().sum()
    pct = missing / n * 100
    summary = pd.DataFrame({
        "dataset": name,
        "variable": missing.index,
        "n_missing": missing.values,
        "pct_missing": pct.values,
    })
    return summary[summary["n_missing"] > 0].sort_values("pct_missing", ascending=False)

summaries = pd.concat([
    missing_summary(baseline,  "baseline"),
    missing_summary(schools,   "schools"),
    missing_summary(math_chat, "math_chat"),
    missing_summary(math_quiz, "math_quiz"),
    missing_summary(voc_chat,  "voc_chat"),
    missing_summary(voc_steps, "voc_steps"),
], ignore_index=True)

# High-level counts
overview = (
    summaries.groupby("dataset")
    .agg(
        vars_any_missing=("variable", "count"),
        vars_gt10pct=("pct_missing", lambda x: (x > 10).sum()),
        vars_gt50pct=("pct_missing", lambda x: (x > 50).sum()),
    )
    .reset_index()
)
print(overview.to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
  dataset  vars_any_missing  vars_gt10pct  vars_gt50pct
 baseline                15            12             9
math_chat                 9             9             8
math_quiz                 1             1             1
  schools                 1             1             1
 voc_chat                 1             1             1
voc_steps                 1             1             0
```
:::
:::


### Missing rate heatmap — Baseline survey

The baseline has the most variables (183). Below we show the 30 variables with the highest missing rates.

::: {#885d8880 .cell execution_count=3}
``` {.python .cell-code}
top_missing_baseline = (
    summaries[summaries["dataset"] == "baseline"]
    .head(30)
    .set_index("variable")["pct_missing"]
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
bars = ax.barh(top_missing_baseline.index[::-1], top_missing_baseline.values[::-1], color="#4C72B0")
ax.axvline(10, color="orange", linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label=">10% threshold")
ax.axvline(50, color="red",    linestyle="--", linewidth=1, label=">50% threshold")
ax.set_xlabel("% Missing")
ax.set_title("Top 30 variables by missing rate — Baseline survey")
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/01_missing_baseline.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](01_data_quality_files/figure-html/cell-4-output-1.png){width=1480 height=881}
:::
:::


## 1.2 Value Validity

### Likert scale ranges (should be 1–5)

::: {#6d5ee9b0 .cell execution_count=4}
``` {.python .cell-code}
scale_cols = utils.SCALE_VARS
validity_rows = []
for col in scale_cols:
    if col in baseline.columns:
        col_data = baseline[col].dropna()
        out_of_range = ((col_data < 1) | (col_data > 5)).sum()
        validity_rows.append({
            "variable": col,
            "min": col_data.min(),
            "max": col_data.max(),
            "out_of_range": out_of_range,
        })

scale_validity = pd.DataFrame(validity_rows)
print(scale_validity.to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
            variable  min  max  out_of_range
            grit_avg  1.0  5.0             0
         mindset_avg  1.0  6.0            92
           locus_avg  1.0  5.0             0
        efficacy_avg  1.0  3.8             0
      math_enjoy_avg  1.0  5.0             0
    math_anxiety_avg  1.0  5.0             0
math_selfconcept_avg  1.0  5.0             0
       belonging_avg  1.0  5.0             0
```
:::
:::


### Binary variables (should be 0 or 1)

::: {#9eb64126 .cell execution_count=5}
``` {.python .cell-code}
binary_cols = ["female", "has_computer", "has_internet", "knows_career",
               "knows_scholarship", "knows_beca18", "enrolled_academy"]

bin_rows = []
for col in binary_cols:
    if col in baseline.columns:
        unique_vals = baseline[col].dropna().unique()
        invalid = [v for v in unique_vals if v not in (0, 1, 0.0, 1.0)]
        bin_rows.append({
            "variable": col,
            "unique_values": sorted(unique_vals),
            "invalid_values": invalid if invalid else "none",
        })

print(pd.DataFrame(bin_rows).to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
         variable unique_values invalid_values
           female        [0, 1]           none
     has_computer        [0, 1]           none
     has_internet        [0, 1]           none
     knows_career     [1, 2, 3]         [3, 2]
knows_scholarship     [1, 2, 3]         [2, 3]
     knows_beca18     [1, 2, 3]         [2, 3]
 enrolled_academy        [0, 1]           none
```
:::
:::


### Platform timestamp checks

Check that timestamps are chronologically plausible (no future dates, no pre-study dates).

::: {#920e2cd5 .cell execution_count=6}
``` {.python .cell-code}
STUDY_START = pd.Timestamp("2023-01-01")
STUDY_END   = pd.Timestamp("2025-12-31")

for name, df, col in [
    ("math_chat", math_chat, "ts_created"),
    ("math_quiz", math_quiz, "ts_created"),
    ("voc_chat",  voc_chat,  "ts_created"),
]:
    ts = pd.to_datetime(df[col], errors="coerce")
    n_null      = ts.isnull().sum()
    n_before    = (ts < STUDY_START).sum()
    n_after     = (ts > STUDY_END).sum()
    print(f"{name}: null={n_null}, before study={n_before}, after study={n_after}")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
math_chat: null=1365, before study=0, after study=13060
math_quiz: null=0, before study=0, after study=5223
voc_chat: null=0, before study=0, after study=5121
```
:::
:::


### Chat message length outliers

::: {#1b17a0c9 .cell execution_count=7}
``` {.python .cell-code}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4))

for ax, (df, label) in zip(axes, [(math_chat, "Math chat"), (voc_chat, "Voc chat")]):
    student_msgs = df[df["is_student"] == 1]["msg_length"].dropna()
    ax.hist(student_msgs.clip(upper=student_msgs.quantile(0.99)), bins=50, color="#4C72B0", edgecolor="white")
    ax.set_title(f"{label} — student message length (clipped at 99th pct)")
    ax.set_xlabel("Characters")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/01_msg_length_dist.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](01_data_quality_files/figure-html/cell-8-output-1.png){width=1653 height=582}
:::
:::


## 1.3 Merge Quality

How many students from the baseline appear in each platform dataset?

::: {#65a5c356 .cell execution_count=8}
``` {.python .cell-code}
baseline_ids  = set(baseline["qualtrics_id"].dropna())
school_ids    = set(schools["school_id"].dropna())
baseline_schools = set(baseline["school_code"].dropna())

# Platform user IDs
math_chat_ids = set(math_chat["user_id"].dropna())
math_quiz_ids = set(math_quiz["user_id"].dropna())
voc_chat_ids  = set(voc_chat["user_id"].dropna())
voc_steps_ids = set(voc_steps["school_user_id"].dropna())

# Coverage relative to baseline
rows = []
for label, platform_ids in [
    ("math_chat (home + school)", math_chat_ids),
    ("math_quiz",                 math_quiz_ids),
    ("voc_chat",                  voc_chat_ids),
    ("voc_steps",                 voc_steps_ids),
]:
    in_both   = len(baseline_ids & platform_ids)
    only_base = len(baseline_ids - platform_ids)
    only_plat = len(platform_ids - baseline_ids)
    rows.append({
        "dataset": label,
        "in_baseline_only": only_base,
        "matched": in_both,
        "in_platform_only": only_plat,
        "baseline_coverage_%": round(in_both / len(baseline_ids) * 100, 1) if baseline_ids else None,
    })

coverage = pd.DataFrame(rows)
print(coverage.to_string(index=False))
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
                  dataset  in_baseline_only  matched  in_platform_only  baseline_coverage_%
math_chat (home + school)              1203        0               987                  0.0
                math_quiz              1203        0               947                  0.0
                 voc_chat              1203        0              1016                  0.0
                voc_steps              1203        0              1177                  0.0
```
:::
:::


::: {#cdf349af .cell execution_count=9}
``` {.python .cell-code}
# School ID cross-validation
in_both   = baseline_schools & school_ids
only_base = baseline_schools - school_ids
only_rand = school_ids - baseline_schools

print(f"School IDs in both baseline and randomization file: {len(in_both)}")
print(f"In baseline only:        {len(only_base)} — {sorted(only_base)}")
print(f"In randomization only:   {len(only_rand)} — {sorted(only_rand)}")
```

### ID coverage bar chart

::: {#049c452a .cell execution_count=10}
``` {.python .cell-code}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 4))
x = range(len(coverage))
ax.bar(x, coverage["baseline_coverage_%"], color="#4C72B0")
ax.set_xticks(list(x))
ax.set_xticklabels(coverage["dataset"], rotation=15, ha="right")
ax.set_ylabel("% of baseline students found in platform")
ax.set_ylim(0, 110)
ax.set_title("Platform coverage of baseline sample")
for i, v in enumerate(coverage["baseline_coverage_%"]):
    ax.text(i, v + 1, f"{v}%", ha="center", fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("../outputs/figures/01_id_coverage.png", dpi=150)
plt.show()
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display}
![](01_data_quality_files/figure-html/cell-11-output-1.png){width=1314 height=581}
:::

## 1.4 Data Quality Summary

::: {#97836569 .cell execution_count=11}
``` {.python .cell-code}
# Scorecard: one row per dataset
scorecard_rows = [
    {
        "Dataset": "baseline_estudiantes",
        "Rows": len(baseline),
        "Variables": len(baseline.columns),
        "Vars >10% missing": (baseline.isnull().mean() > 0.10).sum(),
        "Vars >50% missing": (baseline.isnull().mean() > 0.50).sum(),
        "Key issues": "—",
    },
    {
        "Dataset": "randomizacion_escuelas",
        "Rows": len(schools),
        "Variables": len(schools.columns),
        "Vars >10% missing": (schools.isnull().mean() > 0.10).sum(),
        "Vars >50% missing": (schools.isnull().mean() > 0.50).sum(),
        "Key issues": "—",
    },
    {
        "Dataset": "math_chat (combined)",
        "Rows": len(math_chat),
        "Variables": len(math_chat.columns),
        "Vars >10% missing": (math_chat.isnull().mean() > 0.10).sum(),
        "Vars >50% missing": (math_chat.isnull().mean() > 0.50).sum(),
        "Key issues": "—",
    },
    {
        "Dataset": "math_ponte_prueba",
        "Rows": len(math_quiz),
        "Variables": len(math_quiz.columns),
        "Vars >10% missing": (math_quiz.isnull().mean() > 0.10).sum(),
        "Vars >50% missing": (math_quiz.isnull().mean() > 0.50).sum(),
        "Key issues": "—",
    },
    {
        "Dataset": "voc_chat",
        "Rows": len(voc_chat),
        "Variables": len(voc_chat.columns),
        "Vars >10% missing": (voc_chat.isnull().mean() > 0.10).sum(),
        "Vars >50% missing": (voc_chat.isnull().mean() > 0.50).sum(),
        "Key issues": "—",
    },
    {
        "Dataset": "voc_steps",
        "Rows": len(voc_steps),
        "Variables": len(voc_steps.columns),
        "Vars >10% missing": (voc_steps.isnull().mean() > 0.10).sum(),
        "Vars >50% missing": (voc_steps.isnull().mean() > 0.50).sum(),
        "Key issues": "—",
    },
]
scorecard = pd.DataFrame(scorecard_rows)
print(scorecard.to_string(index=False))
scorecard.to_csv("../outputs/tables/01_data_quality_scorecard.csv", index=False)
```
